g.text: 1. Local development runs Cloudsoft.Web with dotnet run, appsettings.Development.json, local CV storage, and in-memory fallback
2. Azure deployment uses infra/container-apps/main.bicep to create Container Apps, Cosmos DB MongoDB API, Blob Storage, Log
Analytics, Application Insights, ACR, and managed identity.
3. User traffic enters through Azure Container Apps ingress and reaches the Cloudsoft.Web container on the configured application
4. Job and employer data flow through controllers, services, and repositories into Cosmos DB.
5. Candidate CV uploads flow through POST /Job/Apply, JobApplicationService, ICvStorageService, and then into the private cvs
6. Public site assets remain in the images Blob container; private applicant files belong in the cvs container.
7. The Container App managed identity is used for ACR pull and Blob Storage RBAC, avoiding Blob connection strings.
8. The readiness probe calls /healthz. If Cosmos DB or Blob Storage is unavailable, the replica is marked not ready and removed
